response.global_summary: VTuber Yukimi Riona performs energetic 3D stage covers of "Tsumi to Batsu" (originally by Shiina Ringo) and "Ashura-chan" (lyrics and music by Neru, originally by Ado).

scenes.description: Yukimi Riona performs a cover of 'Tsumi to Batsu' on a 3D stage, with dramatic lighting.

scenes.reference_lyrics_en:
Yamate street in the morning stings my cheeks
I throw away an empty pack of cigarettes
Today, once again, I have no place to be
My little room indulges the loneliness I feel
"Don't love my restless shrieking
Don't look to the future
Just live in the moment, something you can be confident in
Call out my name
Touch my body
All I need is this: acknowledge me"
I love you. I'm bawling all alone
And it feels so pointless to be fumbling my way down the road at night
The cheap fluorescent lights at the ticket gate
Don't even cast your shadow
Ah, that warped, distant and uncertain day smells like Seven Stars
Tasting them brings me right back to that time of the year
The fact that I wished for anything at all goes well beyond contradiction
Your voice was more precious to me than anything in the world.
Did I make that hoarse, too...?
A German car and a police car break the silence
Sirens, roaring, the real world, a certain floating...
"Don't love my restless shrieking
Don't look to the future
Just live in the moment, something you can be confident in
Call out my name
Touch my body
All I need is this: acknowledge me"
"Don't love my restless shrieking
Just live in the moment, something you can be confident in
Call out my name
Touch my body
All I need is this: acknowledge me"
Yamate street in the morning stings my cheeks
I throw away an empty pack of cigarettes

response.thoughts:
**Analysis of the VTuber Performance Video**

Okay, so I've got a video here of Yukimi Riona performing, and I need to break down what's happening. First, I'm mapping the scenes in the video. I see two distinct songs: "罪と罰" (Tsumi to Batsu) from 0:00 to 2:18 and "阿修羅ちゃん" (Ashura-chan) from 2:19 to 5:07. It's a quick video – just over five minutes.

Now, let's get into the details for each song. "罪と罰" is clearly a cover of a Shiina Ringo track. I need to note that in the context provided, I'm supposed to use the original composer, not the performer, so Shiina Ringo it is for this one. Next up, "阿修羅ちゃん". The on-screen credits say lyrics and music by Neru. Ado is the artist most associated with it, but I need to focus on Neru for the purpose of this analysis. I almost forgot that instruction. *Correction made*.

Next step, I need to get the lyrics for both songs. My plan is to run some quick searches. I'll get the Japanese lyrics and then an English translation for each, so I'll need to remember to do it this way: initial searches for "[Song Title in OG language] [Original Artist/Composer] [lyrics keyword in OG language]" and "[Romaji or English Title] [Original Artist/Composer] lyrics English". So, that will be: "罪と罰 椎名林檎 歌詞", "Tsumi to Batsu Shiina Ringo lyrics English", "阿修羅ちゃん Neru 歌詞", and "Ashura-chan Ado lyrics English".

Finally, I need to get the output formatted. I have the JSON schema, so that's the easy part. The global summary will be: "VTuber Yukimi Riona performs covers of 'Tsumi to Batsu' (Shiina Ringo) and 'Ashura-chan' (Ado/Neru) on a 3D stage." Then, I have the two scenes: "Scene 1: 00:00 - 02:18, 'Tsumi to Batsu'" and "Scene 2: 02:19 - 05:07, 'Ashura-chan'".

Now, it's time to execute those searches and prepare the output.



**Analysis of Lyrics: A Dual Narrative**

Okay, here's the breakdown, as I see it. I've got the lyrics and translations for both *Tsumi to Batsu* (Sin and Punishment) by Shiina Ringo, performed by Yukimi Riona, and *Ashura-chan* by Neru, also performed by Yukimi Riona. Immediately, the contrast between the two songs stands out.

*   **Tsumi to Batsu:** This is a raw, almost desperate plea. The imagery is visceral – the stinging morning air, the discarded cigarettes. It speaks of a confined space, a room that fosters loneliness. The speaker yearns for acknowledgement, a connection that feels both urgent and perhaps destructive. "Don't love my restless shrieking" is a powerful line. The repetition reinforces the cyclical nature of this craving, a moment defined by the immediate. The mention of the German car and police sirens introduces an unsettling element, a disruption of the fragile equilibrium. There's a feeling of being trapped in a loop of needing someone's attention.

*   **Ashura-chan:** In contrast, this song projects a cynical, almost defiant worldview. The lyrics speak of a world consumed by superficiality, where people chase fleeting pleasures. The speaker sees through this charade and is willing to embrace the "monster" within, the Ashura, the Buddhist deity of wrath. There's a rejection of conventional morality, a willingness to exploit and devour ("Even the devil can be devoured"). The lyrics drip with disdain for those who don't understand, the "you" being addressed seems clueless and out of touch. The constant refrain of "Understand" is dripping with sarcasm. It's about surviving and not being a sheep. "Running away as Buddha" is interesting, as a Buddhist is considered as one who has attained nirvana. In the end, the song's intent is to create a sense of being above the fray, and the speaker is willing to do whatever it takes to stay that way.



Okay, here's my take on those thoughts, tailored for an expert audience:

**Timestamp Adjustment - Second Song**

Alright, I'm taking a look at these timestamps, and it looks like the end timestamp for the second song is off by a hair. It's a minor thing, but accuracy is paramount, especially when dealing with this level of precision. The video's actual end time is at approximately 05:04.988, not that far off, so I'll bring that end timestamp down to match. I'll probably set it to 05:04.000, or something very close to 05:04.988. I'll set the scene end to be the precise video end time, at 05:04.988, for complete alignment. We're dealing with milliseconds here, so it's worth the extra effort to get it right.
